column1: # Most likely alpha chain per DP/DQ beta chain, learned from the IEDB-derived pmhc panel.
# MHC-II is an alpha-beta heterodimer, but many studies type only the beta chain, leaving panel keys
# like "-DPB11101" (2,511 ligands) with no groove and therefore unscorable. DRA is monomorphic so DR
# needs no table (pseudoseq.class2_key hardcodes it); DPA1/DQA1 are polymorphic and must be learned.
# DQA1-DQB1 sit in strong linkage disequilibrium, so the pairing is highly predictable for common
# haplotypes -- this table rediscovers DQ2.5 (DQB1*02:01-DQA1*05:01) and DQ8 (DQB1*03:02-DQA1*03:01)
# Criterion: P(34-mer GROOVE | beta) >= 0.95 over >= 50 fully-typed panel ligands.
# The GROOVE, not the allele name nor its 2-digit group: DQA1*01:02 and DQA1*01:05 share the group
# DQA1*01 but have different 34-mers, so a group-level rule reads 100% certain while the sequence is
# still a 58/42 coin flip -- DQA1 polymorphism sits in the alpha1 domain the pseudosequence samples.
# Measured, not assumed. Rare DQ betas fail the bar and are deliberately left unresolved: a wrong
# groove scores silently, which is worse than not scoring at all.
# alpha is written exactly as it appears in a real key, i.e. keys rebuild as f"{alpha}-{beta}".
